userText: We met strong resistance from user to changed the interface.
correctedText: We met with strong resistance from users after we changed the interface.

issues.note: 'met strong resistance' → 'met WITH strong resistance'; the preposition 'with' is part of the fixed chunk and was dropped for the 2nd time today
issues.suggestion: Drill the full unit: met WITH resistance — say it out loud 5 times: 'met with, met with, met with'

issues.note: 'from user' → 'from users'; when referring to a group of users in general, always use plural
issues.suggestion: users (plural), customers, employees — group nouns are almost always plural when no article is used

issues.note: 'to changed the interface' → 'after we changed the interface'; 'resistance to' takes a noun/gerund (resistance to change), not 'to + past tense verb'
issues.suggestion: For time sequence: use 'after we changed / after the update / when the interface changed' — not 'to changed'
